findings: New file: scripts/keel-dashboard.cjs (CJS, mirrors keel-state.cjs convention). All dashboard logic is isolated here; bin/keel.js gains only three additive changes (ROUTES.dashboard entry, ctx.port field, help-string lines).
bin/keel.js wires the dashboard via the existing run() spawnSync helper — consistent with how keel-state.cjs is already invoked. No ESM/CJS interop problem arises.
keel-state.cjs is structurally guaranteed to be untouched: the design places zero new logic there. AC-7 regression guard is satisfied by file separation, not just by convention.

State reading follows ADR-001 exactly: lock-free, per-entry try/catch (NOT readJson/readManifest which die()), skip-and-mark corrupt manifests as error StoryRows. The HTTP server never crashes on a bad manifest.

PHASE_NAMES is a 12-entry display-label array local to keel-dashboard.cjs. It is parallel to but not shared with AGENTS in keel-state.cjs — sharing would require modifying keel-state.cjs, violating AC-7.

SCOPES constant is also duplicated in keel-dashboard.cjs for the same reason. The engineer must keep them in sync manually; a future refactor could extract shared constants to a third file without touching either existing script.
server.listen binds explicitly to '127.0.0.1' (not 0.0.0.0) to prevent LAN exposure of story metadata. This is a required implementation detail, not optional.
escHtml() is mandatory before any manifest-derived string enters the HTML template. The five characters &, <, >, ", ' must all be escaped to prevent stored-XSS from crafted manifest files.
EADDRINUSE is caught in server.on('error'); message goes to stderr, process exits 1. Other errors re-throw (standard Node.js fatal). No silent swallowing.

Sort order: valid rows sorted by updated_at DESC (most recently active first, per functional spec); error rows sorted last. Comparison uses String.localeCompare on ISO 8601 strings (lexicographic == chronological).

formatIdle formula is verbatim from conventions.md 2026-07-14 and cmdDescribe. Caller must guard absent updated_at before calling (returns 'unknown' if absent). Negative ms should be clamped with Math.max(0, ms) to prevent '-1m 0s' output from clock skew.
generateHTML receives the sorted StoryRow[] and port integer. It is a pure function (no I/O, no side effects) — directly testable without starting an HTTP server.

The only <style> block in the generated HTML contains the @keyframes pulse animation for the live indicator dot. All other CSS is inline style attributes. This is the minimal exception approved in ADR-003.

codegraph.json tracks only PHP classes; JS CLI files have no graph nodes. Manual impact analysis: bin/keel.js is the sole reverse-dependent. The three additive changes to it do not alter any existing ROUTES behavior.

L-1 (KEEL-101 lesson: user-facing strings must not hard-code paths that migrations miss) is satisfied: the startup message 'Dashboard: http://localhost:<port>' contains only a runtime-determined port integer, not a file path.
decisions: Dashboard in new scripts/keel-dashboard.cjs (not inline in bin/keel.js): 300+ line HTML generator + HTTP lifecycle in the CLI dispatcher would conflate two concerns and make the module hard to test from a CJS harness.
Explicit loopback bind ('127.0.0.1'): security requirement — developer-tool server must not be reachable from LAN without explicit intent.
No shared constants with keel-state.cjs: PHASE_NAMES and SCOPES are duplicated to preserve the AC-7 zero-touch guarantee on keel-state.cjs.
escHtml() required on all manifest-sourced strings: cheap XSS defense even though manifests are written by the pipeline engine; defense in depth.
generateHTML is a pure function: enables unit testing of HTML output without an HTTP server; aligns with testability-first design.
Error StoryRows sorted last (not first): an operator scanning the dashboard sees healthy stories first; errors are a secondary concern that do not obscure normal pipeline state.
